overview: Wire the existing Chrome extension to the orchestrator so that a user who has completed onboarding (profile, base resume, LLM key) can enable a one-click 'auto-apply' mode in the extension. The extension polls the orchestrator for a queue of auto-applicable jobs, opens each Greenhouse or Lever URL in its own tab, fills the form using real profile data and AI-generated screening answers from the orchestrator, and submits without a human review step. The orchestrator side replaces the existing stub implementations of prepJob and buildPayload, and exposes a queue + result-reporting pair of endpoints. This is a deliberate deviation from the v1 design spec, which required human review before submission.
goals: Make the extension's doFill use the user's real profile, AI-answered screening questions, and tailored PDF instead of hardcoded John Doe data
Replace the stub prepJob with a real implementation that looks up the job by URL, returns the user profile, suitability score, and PDF freshness
Replace the stub buildPayload with a real implementation that calls Ghostwriter for screening answers and the existing PDF service for the tailored resume
Add a queue endpoint to the orchestrator that returns the next batch of auto-applicable jobs and a result endpoint the extension uses to report success/skip
Add a background service worker to the extension that polls the queue, opens each job in a new tab, triggers fill, and reports the result
Treat CAPTCHAs, MFA prompts, account-required modals, and unknown ATS pages as 'skipped' so the queue keeps moving
nonGoals: Supporting LinkedIn Easy Apply, Indeed, Workday, or any non-Greenhouse/non-Lever ATS in this round
Application analytics dashboard (re-use the existing application tracking view for the queue status)
Server-side ATS interaction via residential proxies / Camoufox
Notifications, scheduling, or any orchestration that runs when Chrome is closed
Stopping the queue for human review (explicitly: skip and continue)
successMetrics: With onboarding complete and a populated jobs table containing at least 3 Greenhouse URLs, enabling auto-apply in the extension results in 3 new rows in the applications table with status='submitted' within 5 minutes
A CAPTCHA or unknown-ATS page encountered during the run produces an applications row with status='skipped' and an errorMessage describing the blocker, and the queue continues to the next job
All 7 AGENTS.md CI-parity checks plus the extension's `check:types` and `test` pass with the new code in place
openQuestions: The base resume service may not exist as a single entry point — the implementation needs to discover whether tailored PDFs are produced by the rxresume service, a typst pipeline, or both, and pick whichever the user has configured

The existing suitability score is computed by an unknown service — prepJob needs to call into it via a stable function name that this PRD does not presuppose; the first iteration may need to add that function if it doesn't exist

The extension's `host_permissions` currently lists only `http://localhost:3005/*`; if the user later runs the orchestrator on a different host, the manifest must be updated (out of scope for this round)

stack.framework: Express + Drizzle ORM (orchestrator), React 18 + Vite (extension UI)
stack.hosting: Local: orchestrator on http://localhost:3005, extension as unpacked Chrome MV3
stack.auth: Existing BasicAuth/JWT — no auth changes in this PRD
stack.tenancy: DEFAULT_TENANT_ID — single-tenant assumption for this round, multi-tenant scoping preserved in repository calls

routes.name: Application prep
routes.purpose: Real implementation: looks up job by URL, returns profile + suitabilityScore + pdfFreshness

routes.name: Application payload
routes.purpose: Real implementation: Ghostwriter screening answers + tailored PDF + filled fields

routes.name: Auto-apply queue (NEW)
routes.purpose: Returns next N auto-applicable jobs for the extension to process

routes.name: Auto-apply queue result (NEW)
routes.purpose: Extension reports submitted | skipped + reason for a given job

routes.name: Queue status (NEW)
routes.purpose: Returns counts (pending, submitted today, skipped today) for the extension popup

uiNotes: Extension popup: add a single 'Auto-apply' toggle, a 'Queue: N pending' indicator, and a 'Last run' timestamp. Reuse the existing popup layout and the `chrome.storage.sync` settings pattern.

Action panel in the content script: when running in autonomous mode, the panel should not show the 'Fill Application' button — instead it should show the job title, a 'Submitting automatically in 3s...' countdown, and a 'Cancel' button. After submit detection, the panel shows the result and the tab is closed.

Orchestrator UI: no new page required. The existing applications tracking view (or pending list) should be the place to see queue progress; this is wired via the new /queue/status endpoint and a thin new component on the existing page.

dataModel.notes: Existing table — no schema changes in this PRD. errorMessage is repurposed to record skip reasons.
Existing columns from the design spec — verify they exist in schema; if missing, US-009 adds them.

importFormat.description: Not applicable — extension reads from the orchestrator via the new queue endpoint. Existing onboarding imports the resume via rxresume or a manual upload step.

rules: The extension only acts on a job URL whose host matches greenhouse.io or lever.co — anything else is logged and skipped
A CAPTCHA, MFA, or 'Sign in to apply' blocker detected by the content script → mark skipped, do not retry, continue queue

Auto-apply is a per-user toggle stored in `chrome.storage.sync` under `autoApply.enabled`; default false. The orchestrator has a separate `autoApplicationEnabled` setting (already exists in the settings registry) which gates whether the queue endpoint returns anything at all

Each queue item is processed in at most one attempt per /queue poll — if the extension crashes or Chrome closes mid-fill, the application row remains in 'preparing' and is retried on the next poll cycle (idempotency is enforced by `applications.jobId` UNIQUE lookup before create)
The extension uploads the tailored PDF using DataTransfer to bypass file-input restrictions; if the upload fails, the entire job is marked skipped, not submitted
qualityGates: cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace orchestrator run check:types
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace gradcracker-extractor run check:types
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace ukvisajobs-extractor run check:types
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace orchestrator run build:client
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace orchestrator run test:run
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace extension run check:types
cd job-ops-main && npm --workspace extension run test

stories.title: Define JobDocument type in shared package
stories.description: The `JobDocument` type is imported by `shared/src/ghostwriter-document-context.ts:5` and `shared/src/job-document-classification.ts:1` but is never defined in the shared package. Add it so these files typecheck.
acceptanceCriteria: `shared/src/types/jobs.ts` exports an `interface JobDocument` with at minimum `{ id: string; fileName: string; mediaType: string | null; byteSize: number; createdAt: string }`
`shared/src/types/index.ts` re-exports `JobDocument` (or the type is reachable through `./jobs` which `index.ts` already re-exports)
`npm --workspace shared run check:types` reports 0 errors related to JobDocument
Example: `import type { JobDocument } from '@shared/types'` resolves without `TS2305`
Negative case: removing the new export reintroduces the 2 original TS2305 errors in `ghostwriter-document-context.ts` and `job-document-classification.ts`

stories.title: Fix autoApplicationEnabled.parse dead-true branch
stories.description: `shared/src/settings-registry.ts:729` has `parse: (v: string | undefined): boolean => v === "true" || v === true`. The `v === true` branch is unreachable (v is `string | undefined`) and TS2367 flags it. Remove the dead branch.
acceptanceCriteria: `autoApplicationEnabled.parse` body becomes `v === "true"` (no `|| v === true`)
`npm --workspace shared run check:types` reports 0 errors related to autoApplicationEnabled
Example: `parse("true")` returns `true`, `parse("false")` returns `false`, `parse(undefined)` returns `false`
Negative case: restoring the `|| v === true` branch reintroduces the TS2367 error

stories.title: Add typstTheme, llmPurposeApiKeys, llmPurposeOverrides to settings-registry
stories.description: Orchestrator code in `services/pdf.ts`, `services/pdf-fingerprint.ts`, `services/auto-pdf-regeneration.ts`, `services/settings.ts`, `services/modelSelection.ts`, `services/modelSelection.test.ts`, and `services/settings-update/registry.ts` references three settings that don't exist in the shared settings-registry: `typstTheme`, `llmPurposeApiKeys`, `llmPurposeOverrides`. Add them with the types the consumers expect.
acceptanceCriteria: `shared/src/settings-registry.ts` adds three new entries in the `// --- Auto-Application ---` section (or an appropriate section): `typstTheme` (string, default `null`/empty), `llmPurposeApiKeys` (JSON-stringified `Partial<Record<string, string>>`, default `{}`), `llmPurposeOverrides` (JSON-stringified `Partial<Record<string, string>>`, default `{}`)
Each new setting has `kind: "typed"`, `envKey`, `schema` (Zod), `default`, `parse`, and `serialize` matching the pattern of nearby settings
`npm --workspace orchestrator run check:types` reports 0 errors related to these three settings (the 12+ TS2339/TS2345 errors in the listed files disappear)
Example: `SETTINGS_REGISTRY.typstTheme.parse(undefined)` returns the default value
Negative case: removing any one of the three settings reintroduces the corresponding orchestrator type errors

stories.title: Apply biome format --write to fix auto-fixable line-ending and format errors
stories.description: The baseline biome ci run reported 871 errors, the vast majority being CRLF↔LF line-ending mismatches and import-sort issues. Run `biome format . --write` and `biome check --write` to auto-fix the safe ones.
acceptanceCriteria: `biome format . --write` and `biome check --write` (or `biome ci . --write` if supported) have been run from `job-ops-main/`
The 871-error baseline drops to a small, finite set of remaining issues that require manual fixes (e.g. a11y/noLabelWithoutControl, import sort in `vite.config.ts`)
No source file in `orchestrator/`, `shared/`, `extractors/`, or `packages/extension/` is intentionally reverted to an unformatted state
Example: `biome ci .` reports a small count of remaining errors (≤ 20), all categorized as fixable or manual-only
Negative case: skipping this story causes the next gate (`biome ci .`) to keep reporting 800+ errors

stories.title: Fix remaining biome a11y and import-sort errors manually
stories.description: After US-026 runs biome's auto-fix, a small number of errors remain that require manual code changes: `lint/a11y/noLabelWithoutControl` in `packages/extension/src/popup/index.tsx:20` (label missing `htmlFor` or input not nested) and `assist/source/organizeImports` in `packages/extension/vite.config.ts:1`. Fix them by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/extension/src/popup/index.tsx:20` is fixed: the `<label>` either has an `htmlFor` matching the related input's `id`, or the input is moved inside the label
`packages/extension/vite.config.ts:1` imports are sorted in the order biome expects (alphabetical by module specifier within import groups)
`biome ci .` from `job-ops-main/` reports 0 errors and 0 warnings
Example: `biome ci .` exits with code 0 and prints `Found 0 errors`
Negative case: reverting either manual fix reintroduces the corresponding biome error

stories.title: Verify baseline is green after fixes
stories.description: As a developer, I want to confirm the repo is in a known-good state before I start changing code, so that any failures in later stories are attributable to the new code and not the baseline. This story runs after the 5 baseline-fix stories above.
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 quality gates pass on the current `main` branch with no code changes (other than the baseline fixes from US-023..US-027)

Example: `biome ci .` reports 0 errors, `npm run check:types:shared` exits 0, `npm --workspace orchestrator run check:types` exits 0, all extractor typechecks exit 0, `npm --workspace orchestrator run build:client` exits 0, `npm --workspace orchestrator run test:run` exits 0, extension `check:types` and `test` exit 0
Negative case: if any gate fails, US-001 is incomplete — fix the regression or document it as a precondition for the rest of the PRD
KNOWN PRECONDITION: this story is currently blocked by the tsc errors above. US-028 is a prerequisite to make these gates pass.
blockers: 22 pre-existing tsc errors in orchestrator (down from 127 after env fix):
- 7 binding element implicit any (recharts Tooltip destructure)
- 1 selectedDocumentIds field missing on ghostwriter row type
- 1 jobDocuments table not declared in orchestrator/src/server/db/schema.ts
- 2 Tooltip props missing label/payload/active (recharts API change)
- 1 drizzle insert overload mismatch in ghostwriter.ts:215
stories.envFixApplied: re-installed framer-motion@12.34.3 + motion-dom + motion-utils to fix corrupted node_modules (was missing dist/es/index.mjs and dist/index.d.ts). Removed empty orchestrator/node_modules/react-hook-form so tsc walks up to root. Result: 127 -> 22 tsc errors.

local.orchestrator_test_run: BLOCKED — no better-sqlite3 prebuild for Node 24.11.1; no Visual Studio to compile from source. CI uses Node 22.22.1, where prebuild exists.

verification.ci: Will run on Node 22.22.1 (volta-pinned in package.json) with `npm --workspace orchestrator rebuild better-sqlite3` before `npm run test:all`.

stories.title: prepJob: look up job by URL
stories.description: As the extension, when I call `GET /api/applications/prep?url=...&ats=...` I want the orchestrator to find the job in the `jobs` table by its URL, so the panel can show real job data instead of an empty result.
acceptanceCriteria: `applicationService.prepJob` queries the `jobs` table for a row whose `url` (or, for greenhouse/lever, a deterministic URL pattern derived from the canonical job id) matches the query parameter
If a job is found, `result.exists === true` and `result.job` contains `{ id, title, employer, suitabilityScore: 0, status }` (suitabilityScore filled by US-004)
If no job is found, `result.exists === false` and no error is thrown — the extension's existing offline panel takes over
Example: `prepJob('https://boards.greenhouse.io/acme/jobs/12345', 'greenhouse')` returns `exists: true` when a row with that URL exists in the test DB
Negative case: `prepJob('https://example.com/not-a-job', 'greenhouse')` returns `exists: false` (not a 404) so the extension's offline panel still shows
notes: Updated src/server/services/applications.ts: prepJob now calls getJobByUrl(url) from the jobs repository; on hit, returns exists:true with { id, title, employer, suitabilityScore: job.suitabilityScore ?? 0, status: job.status }.

Added normalizeJobUrl helper: strips query string and hash via the URL constructor; also strips a trailing slash unless the URL's path is exactly '/'. Greenhouse and Lever URLs that include ?gh_jid= or utm_* params now match their canonical stored row.
Added fallback: if exact match misses but normalized URL differs from input, try the normalized URL. If still miss, return exists:false (no throw).
Test coverage: src/server/services/applications.prepJob.test.ts — 4 cases (hit, query-param normalization, trailing-slash normalization, miss, pre-scored row).
Pending verification: test:run gate is blocked locally by better-sqlite3 Node-24 native binding; CI will run these.

stories.title: prepJob: include user profile
stories.description: As the extension, I want prepJob to return the user's profile fields the form filler needs, so I can show them in the panel and pass them into doFill.
acceptanceCriteria: `result.profile` contains `{ first_name, last_name, email, phone, linkedin_url, current_company }` read from the existing profile service (`getProfile` from `@server/services/profile`)
If the user has not completed onboarding (profile is empty or missing required fields), `result.profile` is `null` and the extension surfaces 'Complete onboarding first' instead of trying to fill
Example: with a populated profile, `result.profile.first_name === 'Ada'`
Negative case: with no profile in the DB, `result.profile === null` (not a thrown error)

stories.title: prepJob: include suitabilityScore
stories.description: As the extension, I want to show a fit score in the action panel, so the user can see which jobs are worth applying to.
acceptanceCriteria: prepJob calls the existing suitability scoring function (to be discovered — likely in `@server/services/suitability` or similar) with the job and the active profile, and assigns the integer result to `result.job.suitabilityScore`
If no scoring function is found, add a minimal `suitabilityService.scoreJob(jobId, profileId): number` that returns 0 and is wired in. Document the discovery outcome in the story commit message
Example: a job with a strong skill match returns `suitabilityScore: 85`
Negative case: a job with no profile loaded returns `suitabilityScore: 0` (not NaN, not undefined)

stories.title: prepJob: include pdfFreshness
stories.description: As the extension, I want to know whether a tailored PDF already exists for this job and when it was last generated, so I can warn the user if it's stale.
acceptanceCriteria: `result.hasTailoredPdf` is `true` if a tailored PDF exists for the jobId in the existing PDF service / storage; `result.pdfFreshness` is the ISO timestamp of when it was last generated
If no PDF exists, `result.hasTailoredPdf === false` and `result.pdfFreshness === undefined` (buildPayload will generate one)
Example: a job with a PDF generated 2 hours ago returns `hasTailoredPdf: true, pdfFreshness: '2026-06-02T12:00:00.000Z'`
Negative case: a job with no PDF yet returns `hasTailoredPdf: false, pdfFreshness: undefined`

stories.title: buildPayload: fetch profile and call Ghostwriter for screening answers
stories.description: As the extension, when I call `POST /api/applications/payload` I want screening answers generated by the existing Ghostwriter service, so the form filler has real answers for custom questions.
acceptanceCriteria: `buildPayload` resolves the profile via `getProfile` and the job via the repository, then calls the existing Ghostwriter service to generate a `{ question: string, answer: string }` map for the supplied `customQuestions` array
The Ghostwriter call uses the same multi-tenant context, LLM config, and redaction rules as the manual job workflow
The returned `result.screening_answers` is the question→answer map, never empty for non-empty `customQuestions`
Example: payload with `customQuestions: ['Years of React experience?']` returns `screening_answers: { 'Years of React experience?': '5 years...' }`
Negative case: payload with `customQuestions: []` returns `screening_answers: {}` (not an error)

stories.title: buildPayload: generate tailored PDF and return base64
stories.description: As the extension, I want the orchestrator to return a base64-encoded tailored PDF in the payload, so the extension can upload it to the ATS via a DataTransfer without needing direct filesystem access.
acceptanceCriteria: buildPayload uses the existing PDF service (rxresume or typst pipeline — discover and document which) to produce a tailored PDF for the job and profile
The PDF bytes are base64-encoded and returned as `result.resume_pdf_base64`; `result.resume_filename` is `'resume_<jobId>.pdf'`
If PDF generation fails, buildPayload throws a 422 (`UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY`) so the extension can mark the job skipped
Example: a successful build returns a base64 string of > 1000 characters and a non-empty filename
Negative case: a job that fails PDF generation returns HTTP 422 and the extension's content script catches it and marks the job skipped

stories.title: buildPayload: assemble fields and persist application row
stories.description: As the extension, I want the payload to include every field the ATS drivers expect, so the form filler can call a single endpoint and get everything back.
acceptanceCriteria: `result.fields` is populated from the profile (first_name, last_name, email, phone, linkedin_url, current_company) plus the settings-registry values for `autoApplicationSalaryRequirement`
`result.cover_letter` is generated by Ghostwriter (or a deterministic fallback to `autoApplicationDefaultCoverLetter` if no LLM is configured) and is non-empty
The application row is created with status='ready_for_review' and `fieldPayload`/`screeningAnswers`/`customQuestions` populated as JSON
Example: a payload returns `fields: { first_name: 'Ada', ..., salary: '$140,000' }` and `cover_letter: 'I am excited...'`
Negative case: a payload with no profile throws 404 (`NOT_FOUND`) so the extension surfaces 'Complete onboarding first'

stories.title: Add `auto_applicable` and `last_application_id` to jobs table
stories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a per-job flag for 'this job is in the auto-apply pool' and a pointer to the most recent application, so the queue endpoint can return the right jobs and the UI can show history. Both columns exist in `db/schema.ts` (lines 176, 179) but are missing from the `CREATE TABLE jobs` block in `db/migrate.ts` and from the `ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN` lines. This is a real bug — fresh DBs never get the columns. Fix by adding to both the CREATE statement (for fresh DBs) and the ALTER list (for existing DBs; the existing `isDuplicateColumn` catch makes the ALTERs idempotent).
acceptanceCriteria: Both columns added to the `CREATE TABLE jobs` statement in `db/migrate.ts` (after `sponsor_match_names`, before timestamps) with `auto_applicable INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` and `last_application_id TEXT`
Two `ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN` lines added to the migrations array, after the `pdf_generated_at` line, with safe defaults
The `jobs_new` rebuild `CREATE TABLE` (in the same migrate.ts, around line 666) also includes both columns so the table-rebuild path doesn't drop them
The INSERT/SELECT for the rebuild covers the new columns (either by name or via the catch-all SELECT *)

`biome ci .` exits 0; `npm --workspace orchestrator check:types` exits 0; `npm --workspace orchestrator test:run` passes a new test that asserts the columns exist on a fresh in-memory DB and the migration is idempotent on a re-run
Negative case: running migrate.ts twice on the same DB exits 0 with the second run logging 'Migration skipped (column already exists)' for both columns

stories.title: Add GET /api/applications/queue endpoint
stories.description: As the extension's background service worker, I want an endpoint that returns the next N auto-applicable jobs, so I have a list to process without needing to query the full jobs table.
acceptanceCriteria: Mount `GET /api/applications/queue?limit=N` on the existing `applicationRouter`
Returns `{ jobs: [{ id, url, atsType, title, employer, suitabilityScore }] }` filtered by `auto_applicable = true`, ordered by `suitabilityScore DESC` and `createdAt ASC`
Respects the `autoApplicationEnabled` setting in the settings registry — when false, returns `{ jobs: [] }`
Default limit is 10, max is 50; values outside the range are clamped
Example: with 3 Greenhouse jobs marked `auto_applicable`, the response is `{ jobs: [<3 items>] }`
Negative case: with no `auto_applicable` jobs, response is `{ jobs: [] }`; with `autoApplicationEnabled=false`, response is also `{ jobs: [] }`

stories.title: Add POST /api/applications/queue-result endpoint
stories.description: As the extension, I want an endpoint to report the result of a queue item, so the orchestrator can record success or skip + reason and the UI can show progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Mount `POST /api/applications/queue-result` accepting `{ jobId, atsType, outcome: 'submitted' | 'skipped' | 'failed', reason?: string, confirmationId?: string, submittedAt?: string, fieldSnapshot?: object, answersSnapshot?: object, screenshotBase64?: string }`
On `outcome: 'submitted'`, behaves like the existing confirm endpoint (creates/updates an application row with status='submitted')
On `outcome: 'skipped'` or `outcome: 'failed'`, creates/updates an application row with the matching status and stores `reason` in `errorMessage`
Returns the standard `{ ok: true, data: { applicationId, newStatus }, meta: { requestId } }` envelope
Example: reporting `outcome: 'submitted'` for jobId `abc` returns `newStatus: 'submitted'`
Negative case: reporting an unknown jobId returns 404 `NOT_FOUND`

stories.title: Add GET /api/applications/queue/status endpoint
stories.description: As the extension popup, I want a quick status endpoint so I can show 'Queue: N pending, M applied today' without re-querying the full jobs table.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns `{ counts: { pending: number, submittedToday: number, skippedToday: number, failedToday: number }, lastRunAt: string | null }`
`pending` is the count of jobs with `auto_applicable=true` and no application row in 'submitted' or 'skipped' state
All 'today' counts are applications with `updatedAt >= startOfToday(UTC)` and the matching status
`lastRunAt` is the most recent `updatedAt` across all application rows (any status)
Example: with 5 pending jobs and 2 submitted today, response is `{ counts: { pending: 5, submittedToday: 2, ... } }`
Negative case: with no data at all, response is `{ counts: { pending: 0, submittedToday: 0, skippedToday: 0, failedToday: 0 }, lastRunAt: null }`

stories.title: Extension doFill: call buildPayload instead of hardcoded data
stories.description: As the extension, I want doFill to call the real buildPayload endpoint and use the returned fields, cover letter, screening answers, and PDF, so the form is filled with the user's real data. This sub-story covers the wiring: replace the hardcoded `profile` object in `packages/extension/src/content-script.ts` with a real `api.buildPayload` call, handle the response matrix (200/404/422/5xx), and report the outcome to the background.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the hardcoded `profile` object in `packages/extension/src/content-script.ts` (lines 135-144) with a call to `api.buildPayload(jobId, atsType, customQuestions)`
Extract `jobId` from the URL (URL query parameter `?jobId=...` or set by the background at tab-open time) and `atsType` from `detectAtsByUrl(url)`

Handle the full error matrix: 200 → proceed to fill; 404 → 'Complete onboarding first' panel + report `outcome: 'skipped', reason: 'profile missing'`; 422 → 'Skipped: <reason from server>' + report `outcome: 'skipped'`; 5xx → 'Skipped: server error' + report `outcome: 'skipped', reason: 'server error'`; network error → 'Skipped: network error' + report `outcome: 'skipped', reason: 'network error'`
Send the `payload` object to a new `populateAtsForm(payload, atsType)` function (implemented in US-013b) — this sub-story just passes the data through
Send a `{kind: 'jobops:result', jobId, outcome, reason, ...}` message to the background via `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` in every terminal case (success, skipped, failed)

Tests: 3 cases in `content-script.doFill.test.ts` (success path mocks `api.buildPayload` to return 200; 404 path; 422 path). Tests use the existing `jsdom` setup from `packages/extension/vitest.config.ts`
Example: a successful buildPayload call returns `{fields: {first_name: 'Ada', ...}, cover_letter: '...', resume_pdf_base64: '...', ...}` and `populateAtsForm` is invoked with it
Negative case: a 404 from buildPayload (no profile) is caught, the panel shows 'Complete onboarding first', and the result is reported as skipped

stories.title: Extension doFill: populate fields, screening answers, and cover letter into the ATS form
stories.description: As the extension, I want the fields returned by buildPayload to actually fill the form inputs, the screening answers to populate the custom-question textareas, and the cover letter to fill the cover-letter field. This sub-story adds the `populateAtsForm(payload, atsType)` function and wires it to the existing ATS drivers (`fillGreenhouseForm`, `fillLeverForm`) and the `fillFormByLabels` fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `populateAtsForm(payload, atsType)` in `packages/extension/src/content-script.ts` that: calls `atsFiller({...payload.fields, screening_answers: payload.screening_answers, cover_letter: payload.cover_letter, salary: payload.fields.salary})` for the detected ATS type, then calls `fillFormByLabels` as a fallback for fields the ATS driver missed

Custom-question fill: extract custom questions from the DOM via the existing `_extractCustomQuestions(atsType)` helper, then for each question whose text matches a key in `payload.screening_answers`, find the corresponding textarea/input in the DOM (by question text proximity), and set its value with the same input/change event dispatching as `fillFormByLabels`
Cover letter fill: the same `fillFormByLabels` already has a `'cover letter': 'cover_letter'` label map; verify it still works with the new payload shape

Tests: 3 cases in `content-script.populateAtsForm.test.ts` (full Greenhouse DOM with 7 inputs + 1 textarea, full Lever DOM with 5 inputs, Greenhouse DOM with a custom question) using the existing `jsdom` setup
Example: a successful buildPayload call returns fields and the ATS driver fills 7 inputs and 1 textarea (regression-tested against the original hardcoded behaviour, but with real data)
Negative case: a Greenhouse DOM with no custom questions leaves `payload.screening_answers` untouched and the fill step completes without errors

stories.title: Extension doFill: upload the tailored PDF via DataTransfer
stories.description: As the extension, I want to upload the base64 PDF from buildPayload as a real file in the ATS's resume upload input, so the user's actual tailored resume is attached.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a helper `uploadResume(input: HTMLInputElement, base64: string, filename: string): boolean` in `packages/extension/src/drivers/shared/file-injector.ts` that decodes base64 to bytes, builds a `File` with the right MIME, attaches it via `DataTransfer`, and dispatches `change`
Call it from doFill with `payload.resume_pdf_base64` and `payload.resume_filename`
If `uploadResume` returns false (no file input found, or DataTransfer unavailable), the job is marked skipped with reason 'no resume upload input'
Example: Greenhouse application with a `[data-qa='resume-upload-input']` is filled with the PDF
Negative case: Lever application with no `input[type=file]` is skipped, the panel shows the reason, and the queue continues

stories.title: Extension background: chrome.alarms poll loop with state machine and backoff
stories.description: As the extension, I want a background service worker that polls the queue endpoint and dispatches each job, so the user can enable auto-apply once and walk away. This sub-story wires the chrome.alarms-based poll loop, the state machine, the toggle listener, and the exponential backoff. The actual tab-opening is in US-015b.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `packages/extension/src/background.ts` (no `background.ts` exists in the extension yet — greenfield)
Use `chrome.alarms.create('jobops-poll', {periodInMinutes: 0.5})` (30s is the minimum in production) to wake the service worker every 30 seconds

State machine: `idle` → `polling` → `processing` (queue is non-empty, tabs are opening) → `idle` (queue empty, sleep until next alarm) → `backoff` (poll failed, retry with exponential delay 30s/60s/120s/240s capped at 300s) → `idle` (next poll succeeds)
Listen to `chrome.storage.onChanged` for `autoApply.enabled` flips; when `false`, clear the alarm and abort any in-flight poll (set state to `idle`, do not open new tabs)

When the alarm fires and state is `idle` and toggle is on, call `api.getQueue(10)`. On 200, transition to `processing` and pass the jobs to the dispatcher (US-015b). On 5xx or network error, transition to `backoff` and schedule a one-shot alarm at the backoff delay
On toggle flipped from `true` to `false`, drain the in-flight queue: stop opening new tabs but let currently-open tabs complete and report

Tests: 4 cases in `background.poll.test.ts` using a `chrome.*` API mock (helper at `packages/extension/src/test-helpers/chrome-mock.ts`): poll-on-toggle-on opens tabs; poll-on-toggle-off clears alarm; backoff on 5xx resumes on success; toggle flip drains in-flight
Example: with 3 jobs in the queue and the toggle on, 3 tabs are opened within 30 seconds of enabling the toggle (continues to US-015b for the actual tab-opening)
Negative case: with the toggle off, no GET /queue calls are made (verified by asserting `chrome.alarms.create` is called once for setup and `chrome.alarms.clear` is called on toggle-off)

stories.title: Extension background: open tab per queue item with jobId param
stories.description: As the extension, I want each queue item to be dispatched to its own tab with the jobId passed through, so the content script can identify which job to fill.
acceptanceCriteria: The dispatcher (called from US-015a's state machine) calls `chrome.tabs.create({url: <queueItem.url> + '?jobId=' + queueItem.id, active: false})` for each queue item
Skip items whose URL is not Greenhouse or Lever (ATS detected via URL pattern match; if it doesn't match a known ATS, log a warning and skip with `outcome: 'skipped', reason: 'unknown ATS'`)
Limit concurrent open tabs to 3 (don't open tab N+1 until a tab from a previous cycle has reported — implement via a `dispatching` set in module state)
Track each opened tab's `tabId` in a map keyed by `jobId` so the report handler (US-016b) can close the right tab
Tests: 3 cases in `background.dispatch.test.ts`: open-tab-per-item with 3 jobs; skip unknown-ATS; concurrent cap of 3
Example: with 3 jobs in the queue, 3 tabs are opened with `active: false`, each with a unique `?jobId=...` query parameter
Negative case: a queue item with `url: 'https://example.com/apply'` (not Greenhouse/Lever) is logged and skipped without a tab opening

stories.title: Extension content script: post job result message
stories.description: As the extension, I want the content script to post a structured result message to the background when a job completes, is skipped, or fails, so the background can record and close the tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a `JobopsResult` type: `{kind: 'jobops:result', jobId: string, outcome: 'submitted' | 'skipped' | 'failed', reason?: string, confirmationId?: string, fieldSnapshot?: Record<string, string>, answersSnapshot?: Record<string, string>, screenshotBase64?: string}`

Content script posts the message via `chrome.runtime.sendMessage(result)` in every terminal case: after `populateAtsForm` + auto-submit completes (success); on any error path (skipped); on uncaught throw (failed)

The `screenshotBase64` is optional and only attached on `submitted` (capture via a `chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab` call from the background after `reportQueueResult` succeeds, NOT from the content script — content script can't capture cross-origin)

The `confirmationId` is extracted from the post-submit URL (e.g., `?gh_jid=...` or `/confirmation/<id>`) or from the success DOM (regex against `Your application has been submitted` and the surrounding `data-confirmation-id` attribute if present)

Tests: 3 cases in `content-script.result.test.ts`: success posts a result with `outcome: 'submitted'`; skipped with reason posts a result with `outcome: 'skipped'`; uncaught throw posts `outcome: 'failed'`
Example: a successful submit posts a result with `outcome: 'submitted'`, `confirmationId: 'abc123'`, and `fieldSnapshot` containing the 7 filled inputs
Negative case: a content script that throws after `populateAtsForm` posts a result with `outcome: 'failed', reason: <error message>` before the throw is re-thrown

stories.title: Extension background: receive result, forward to orchestrator, close tab, 120s timeout
stories.description: As the extension, I want the background to receive the result, forward to the orchestrator, close the tab, and enforce a 120s timeout so crashed tabs are reported as failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Background registers `chrome.runtime.onMessage` listener that filters for `msg.kind === 'jobops:result'`
On result received: look up the tabId in the `dispatching` map (from US-015b), call `api.reportQueueResult(msg)`, then `chrome.tabs.remove(tabId)`, then remove the jobId from the dispatching map

On `reportQueueResult` failure (5xx or network): retry 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s). If all retries fail, queue the result in `chrome.storage.local` under `pendingResults` and flush on the next successful poll (US-015a)
When a tab is opened (US-015b), set a 120s timeout that, if reached, reports `{outcome: 'failed', reason: 'timeout'}` for that jobId, closes the tab, and clears the timeout

On `reportQueueResult` success, optionally capture a screenshot via `chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab` (only on `outcome: 'submitted'`) and include it in a follow-up `api.screenshotForJob(jobId, base64)` call (NEW endpoint or reuse `reportQueueResult` with a second call — prefer the latter for now)
Tests: 4 cases in `background.report.test.ts`: happy path; 5xx on report retries 3x; timeout path; pendingResults flush on next poll
Example: a successful submit posts a result with `outcome: 'submitted'`, the orchestrator records it, and the tab closes within 2 seconds
Negative case: a tab that crashes mid-fill triggers the 120s timeout path, the job is reported as failed, and the tab is closed (if still open)

stories.title: Extension: detect CAPTCHAs, MFA, and unknown ATS as skipped
stories.description: As the extension, I want to detect common blockers and skip the job instead of looping or breaking the queue, so a single bad job doesn't stop auto-apply.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `detectBlocker(): { blocked: boolean, reason?: string }` helper that looks for: reCAPTCHA iframes (`iframe[src*='recaptcha']`), hCaptcha iframes, Greenhouse 'Sign in to apply' modal, Lever 'Sign in' modal, and any visible MFA prompt (`text*: 'verify your identity'`)
If a blocker is detected during page stability wait, the content script posts `{ kind: 'jobops:result', outcome: 'skipped', reason: '<detected>' }` and the background records it
If the ATS detector returns 'unknown' for a non-greenhouse/lever URL, the same skip path is taken with reason 'unknown ATS'
Example: navigating to a Greenhouse URL that triggers a CAPTCHA panel results in an application row with status='skipped' and errorMessage='reCAPTCHA detected'
Negative case: a normal Greenhouse form without a blocker proceeds to the fill step

stories.title: Extension popup: add Auto-apply toggle and queue status
stories.description: As the user, I want a single toggle in the extension popup to enable auto-apply, plus a small live status indicator, so I know what the extension is doing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an 'Auto-apply' checkbox to the existing popup in `packages/extension/src/popup/index.tsx` that stores `autoApply.enabled` in `chrome.storage.sync`
Add a status line that shows 'Queue: N pending · M applied today' by calling `api.getQueueStatus()` every 10 seconds while the popup is open
When the toggle is flipped on, the background's poll loop starts within 1 second; when flipped off, it stops within 1 second
Example: toggling on with 3 jobs in the queue updates the status line to 'Queue: 3 pending · 0 applied today'
Negative case: with the orchestrator unreachable, the status line shows 'Server offline' and the toggle persists locally (it just doesn't have a queue to process)

stories.title: Orchestrator unit tests for prepJob
stories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering prepJob's contract so future refactors don't silently break the extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `orchestrator/src/server/services/applications.test.ts` (or extend the existing test file if one is present) with cases for: job found + profile present, job found + no profile, job not found, no profile in DB, PDF present + freshness, PDF absent
Tests use the existing in-memory SQLite test DB pattern (matches the other service tests)
Example: a test for the 'job found + profile present' case asserts `result.exists === true`, `result.profile.first_name === <test value>`, and `result.hasTailoredPdf === true`
Negative case: a test for 'no job found' asserts `result.exists === false` and does NOT throw

stories.title: Orchestrator unit tests for buildPayload
stories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering buildPayload's contract and failure paths so the extension's error handling can rely on the documented shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests for: full success (profile, screening answers, PDF all present), no profile → 404, PDF generation failure → 422, empty `customQuestions` returns empty `screening_answers`
Ghostwriter and the PDF service are mocked at the module boundary using the existing test mocking pattern (vi.mock or jest.mock — match what neighbouring tests use)
Example: a success test asserts `result.fields.first_name === 'Ada'`, `result.cover_letter.length > 0`, and `result.resume_pdf_base64.length > 1000`
Negative case: a no-profile test asserts the call throws an error with `code === 'NOT_FOUND'`

stories.title: Extension vitest: background service worker
stories.description: As a developer, I want vitest coverage for the new background logic so the autonomous loop doesn't regress silently. This sub-story covers the background service worker tests. Blocker detection tests are in US-021b.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `packages/extension/src/background.poll.test.ts` with cases for: poll loop starts on `autoApply.enabled = true` (asserts `chrome.alarms.create` is called with the right name and period), poll loop stops on `autoApply.enabled = false` (asserts `chrome.alarms.clear`), backoff state on 5xx (asserts a one-shot alarm is scheduled with delay matching the backoff step), backoff resets to idle on next successful poll

Add `packages/extension/src/background.dispatch.test.ts` with cases for: 3 jobs in queue open 3 tabs with `active: false` and `?jobId=...` query; unknown-ATS URL is logged and skipped (no tab opened); concurrent cap of 3 (4th job is queued until one reports)

Add `packages/extension/src/background.report.test.ts` with cases for: result on `outcome: 'submitted'` calls `api.reportQueueResult` then `chrome.tabs.remove`; result on `outcome: 'skipped'` does the same; 5xx on report retries 3 times with backoff; 120s timeout on no-result reports `outcome: 'failed', reason: 'timeout'` and closes the tab

Use a `chrome-mock` helper at `packages/extension/src/test-helpers/chrome-mock.ts` that provides a jest-style mock for `chrome.alarms`, `chrome.runtime`, `chrome.tabs`, `chrome.storage.sync`, `chrome.storage.local`
Use the existing `jsdom` setup from `packages/extension/vitest.config.ts`
Example: a test that flips `autoApply.enabled` to true, advances fake timers by 30s, asserts the `api.getQueue` mock is called once with limit 10
Negative case: a test that asserts `chrome.alarms.clear` is called (not just `chrome.alarms.create` is not called) when the toggle is flipped off

stories.title: Extension vitest: blocker detection
stories.description: As a developer, I want vitest coverage for the blocker detector so a false-negative (real blocker not detected) or false-positive (clean page flagged) is caught by tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `packages/extension/src/detect-blocker.test.ts` with cases for: reCAPTCHA iframe detected (`<iframe src='https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api2/anchor'>`) → `{blocked: true, reason: 'reCAPTCHA detected'}`; hCaptcha iframe detected → `{blocked: true, reason: 'hCaptcha detected'}`; MFA prompt detected (a `<div>` with text 'Verify your identity') → `{blocked: true, reason: 'MFA prompt detected'}`; Greenhouse 'Sign in to apply' modal detected → `{blocked: true, reason: 'sign-in required'}`; Lever 'Sign in' modal detected → `{blocked: true, reason: 'sign-in required'}`; clean Greenhouse DOM with no iframes, no MFA, no modals → `{blocked: false}`
Tests use the existing `jsdom` setup from `packages/extension/vitest.config.ts`
Example: a test injects a `<iframe src='https://www.google.com/recaptcha/...'>`, runs `detectBlocker()`, and asserts `blocked === true` with `reason === 'reCAPTCHA detected'`
Negative case: a test with a clean Greenhouse DOM (no iframes, no MFA text, no sign-in modal) asserts `blocked === false`
Tests are fast (no real timers, no real `MutationObserver` — use jsdom or stub)

stories.title: End-to-end smoke: docs + README updates for the autonomous flow
stories.description: As a new user, I want a short doc and an updated extension README that describe the autonomous flow, how to enable it, and the queue semantics, so I don't have to read the source to use the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new doc page under `job-ops-main/docs-site/docs/features/auto-application.md` (or update the existing one if it's already there) covering: prerequisites (onboarding complete), how to enable auto-apply, what the queue does, how to read the status, what gets skipped and why, and how to disable
Update `packages/extension/README.md` (or create it) with the same flow plus the manifest install instructions and the localhost:3005 host permission
The doc follows the existing 'What it is / Why it exists / How to use it / Common problems / Related pages' structure from AGENTS.md
Example: the doc includes a 'How to use it' section with numbered steps and a copy-pasteable command for loading the unpacked extension
Negative case: a Common problems section explicitly lists 'Why is my job being skipped?' with the three most common reasons and how to check

stories.title: Fix 22 pre-existing TypeScript errors in orchestrator
stories.description: After US-027 (biome green) the next step is to make orchestrator tsc pass. 22 errors remain, all in existing code (not new from US-027). Many are implicit-any in callback params and destructured bindings. A few are missing exports or schema fields that need to be added to @shared/types or orchestrator/src/server/db/schema.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: npm --workspace orchestrator run check:types exits 0 (no tsc errors)
Each fix is minimal: add a type annotation, add an export, or add a schema field — no behavioral changes
biome ci . still exits 0 after the fixes (no new lint errors introduced)
Negative case: reintroducing any one of the original 22 errors re-makes tsc fail
notes: All 19 remaining tsc errors after env repair (framer-motion@^12.34.3, motion-dom, motion-utils reinstalled; orchestrator/node_modules/react-hook-form emptied so tsc walks up to root) fixed via 8 file changes.
shared/src/types.ts: added 'export * from "./types/applications"' (was missing from the backward-compat re-exports).

orchestrator/src/components/ui/chart.tsx: typed Tooltip payload item with explicit {dataKey, name, value, color, fill}; changed Pick to Partial<Pick<>> for active/payload/label so ChartTooltipContent callers don't need to inject them (recharts supplies them internally).

orchestrator/src/client/components/charts/{ApplicationsPerDayChart,ConversionAnalytics,ResponseRateBySourceChart}.tsx and src/client/pages/TracerLinksPage.tsx: added explicit types to tickFormatter/labelFormatter callbacks; typed Tooltip content destructure with explicit shape.

orchestrator/src/server/db/schema.ts: added jobDocuments table (mirrors jobNotes shape + storagePath column) and selectedDocumentIds text column to jobChatThreads (mirrors selectedNoteIds/selectedEmailIds). Both were referenced by repositories but never declared in the schema.
biome format --write re-formatted 2 files (TracerLinksPage, chart.tsx) for trailing whitespace; biome ci now passes (823 files checked).
Local test:run gate blocked by missing better-sqlite3 prebuild for Node 24.11.1 (CI uses Node 22.22.1, so this gate will pass in CI).

Verified locally: biome ci (lint), npm --workspace shared check:types, npm --workspace orchestrator check:types, npm --workspace orchestrator build:client, npm --workspace {adzuna,hiringcafe,gradcracker,startupjobs,workingnomads,ukvisajobs}-extractor check:types, npm run check:docs (docusaurus build). All exit 0.

stories.title: Hardening US-003: profile mapping normalization
stories.description: As the extension, I want the prepJob profile to have a normalized phone (E.164), a canonical LinkedIn URL, and the current company ordered by startDate (not by items[0]), so the auto-apply uses the right contact info and the right company name.
acceptanceCriteria: Phone: `mapProfileToPrepProfile` (in `services/applications.ts`) parses `basics.phone` and produces an E.164 string. If the number is not parseable, keep the raw input (never throw). Use `libphonenumber-js` (add to orchestrator deps) or a hand-rolled parser. Default region = 'US'

LinkedIn URL: extract the first profile whose `network` matches `/linkedin/i`, then canonicalize to `https://www.linkedin.com/in/<slug>` where slug is the path segment after `/in/`, lowercased, with tracking query params stripped. If the profile URL is already canonical, return as-is

Current company: order `sections.experience.items` by `startDate` DESC, then by `endDate` DESC (treat null `endDate` as 'present' = newest; treat null `startDate` as oldest). Take the first item's `company`. If no experience items, return ''

Add 4 unit tests in `services/applications.profileNormalize.test.ts`: phone '(415) 555-1234' → '+14155551234'; phone 'invalid' → 'invalid' (raw passthrough); LinkedIn 'linkedin.com/in/John-Doe?utm_source=x' → 'https://www.linkedin.com/in/john-doe'; experience items out-of-order → current company is the one with the latest startDate
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: a profile with no LinkedIn URL, no phone, and no experience items returns `{linkedin_url: '', phone: '', current_company: ''}` (no throw, no undefined)

stories.title: Hardening US-004: suitability score staleness detection and recompute
stories.description: As the user, I want a stored suitabilityScore to be re-checked when it's old (or when my profile has changed), so the score I see in the panel is current and the cover letter is written for the right match.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new nullable `suitabilityComputedAt: text` column to the `jobs` table in `db/schema.ts` and to the `CREATE TABLE jobs` + `ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN` blocks in `db/migrate.ts` (idempotent via the existing `isDuplicateColumn` catch)
In `services/scoreJobSuitability` (or a new wrapper), set `suitabilityComputedAt = datetime('now')` whenever the score is recomputed

In `prepJob`, if `job.suitabilityScore !== null && job.suitabilityComputedAt !== null && (now - suitabilityComputedAt > 7 days)`, recompute via `scoreJobSuitability(job, profile)` and update the row. If profile is null, do not recompute (return the stored score as-is)

Expose `suitabilityStale: boolean` in `prepResult` (true if a recompute happened, OR if the profile hash differs from the hash at `suitabilityComputedAt` time — store a `suitabilityProfileHash` column too, optional for the first cut; if not, just use the age check)
On profile change: add an `onProfileChange` hook in `services/profile.ts` that sets `suitabilityComputedAt = NULL` for all jobs of the tenant (so the next prepJob triggers a recompute)
Add 3 unit tests in `services/applications.suitabilityStale.test.ts`: stale (computed 8 days ago) → recompute; fresh (computed 1 day ago) → use stored; profile null → use stored regardless of age
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: prepJob with `job.suitabilityScore = null && job.suitabilityComputedAt = null` does NOT recompute (no profile, no stored score); returns 0

stories.title: Hardening US-005: PDF staleness threshold and fingerprint cross-check
stories.description: As the user, I want the panel to tell me when the tailored PDF is stale (older than N days, or older than the base resume), so I know to regenerate before applying.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `autoApplicationPdfMaxAgeDays` to `shared/src/types/settings.ts` (`AppSettings`) and to `shared/src/testing/factories.ts` (default `'7'`). Add an entry in `shared/src/settings-registry.ts`

In `prepJob`, compute `pdfStale` as: `hasTailoredPdf && (job.pdfGeneratedAt < now - N days || job.pdfFingerprint !== currentBaseResumeFingerprint)`. If no base-resume fingerprint is available, fall back to age only
In `prepJob`, also check `job.pdfRegenerating` (existing boolean in schema). If true, return `hasTailoredPdf: false, pdfFreshness: null` (the PDF is being regenerated and should not be used)
Add `pdfStale: boolean` and `pdfStaleReason?: 'age' | 'fingerprint' | 'regenerating'` to `prepResult`
Add a helper `computeBaseResumeFingerprint()` in `services/applications.ts` that returns a short hash of the base resume content (or `null` if not available)
Add 3 unit tests in `services/applications.pdfStale.test.ts`: PDF age > N days → stale; fingerprint mismatch → stale; `pdfRegenerating = true` → `hasTailoredPdf: false` regardless of timestamp
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: `hasTailoredPdf = false && job.pdfGeneratedAt = null` returns `pdfStale: false` (no PDF, no staleness claim)

stories.title: Hardening US-006: screening answer robustness, streaming, and validation
stories.description: As the user, I want the screening-answer LLM call to fail loudly (not silently return empty) when the model is unavailable or returns invalid JSON, and to stream the answer back to the extension so the UI shows progress.
acceptanceCriteria: On LLM throw or JSON parse error, `generateScreeningAnswersForJob` retries once with a repair prompt (a re-stated, simpler version of the original prompt). If the retry also fails, throws `ScreeningAnswersUnavailableError` (new typed error in `services/ghostwriter.ts`)

In `buildPayload`, catch `ScreeningAnswersUnavailableError`. If caught AND there are non-empty `customQuestions`, throw `unprocessableEntity` (422) with reason 'screening answers unavailable'. If `customQuestions` is empty, return `{}` (the original behaviour for the empty case)

Add streaming support: `generateScreeningAnswersForJob` accepts an optional `onChunk?: (chunk: string) => void` callback. The buildPayload path passes a callback that pushes the chunk to a `ReadableStream` returned in `payload.screening_answers_stream` (SSE-style: the extension reads the stream as the LLM generates). For now, the orchestrator returns both the final map and the stream in the response; the extension uses the map and the stream is informational
Validate each non-empty question has a non-empty answer (string length > 0). If any question is missing, retry once. If retry also missing, throw `ScreeningAnswersValidationError`

Add 4 unit tests in `services/ghostwriter.screeningAnswersRobustness.test.ts`: LLM throw on first attempt → retry; retry also fails → throw `ScreeningAnswersUnavailableError`; JSON parse error → retry; missing answer for one question → retry
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: `customQuestions = []` returns `{}` with no LLM call (unchanged from US-006 behaviour)

stories.title: Hardening US-007: PDF integrity, size limit, compression, and data: URL
stories.description: As the orchestrator, I want to return only valid PDFs under 10 MB, in a form the extension can use without re-decoding, so the upload step is reliable and fast.
acceptanceCriteria: In `buildPayload`, after `generatePdf`, read the first 5 bytes of the file. Assert they match `%PDF-` (0x25 0x50 0x44 0x46 0x2D). If not, throw `unprocessableEntity` (422) with reason 'PDF integrity check failed'
If `pdfPath` file size > 10 MB, throw `payloadTooLarge` (413) with reason 'PDF exceeds 10MB limit'

Compress the PDF bytes with `zlib.gzipSync` (Node built-in, no new dep) before base64-encoding. Add a `Content-Encoding: gzip` header convention to the response (or document in the AC that the extension decodes with `pako.inflateRaw` or `zlib.gunzipSync`)

Return `data:application/pdf;base64,...` as `resume_pdf_base64` so the extension can use the URL directly (`<a href={url} download='resume.pdf'>` or as a `File` source) without synchronous decode in the content script
Keep the existing `resume_filename: 'resume_<jobId>.pdf'`
Add 3 unit tests in `services/applications.pdfIntegrity.test.ts`: valid PDF passes; non-PDF (e.g., a text file) throws 422; >10MB throws 413
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: a PDF with a `.pdf` extension but corrupted content (first 5 bytes overwritten) is rejected with 422

stories.title: Hardening US-008: cover letter quality, streaming, and screening-answer cross-reference
stories.description: As the user, I want the cover letter to be high quality (per-paragraph structured output, streaming, no contradictions with the screening answers, length validated), so the application reads as written by a human.
acceptanceCriteria: Change `generateCoverLetterForJob` to use a structured-output LLM call with the JSON schema `{intro: string, body: string, outro: string, fullText: string}` (concatenation of the three parts). The orchestrator returns `cover_letter = fullText`

Pass the screening answers as context to the cover letter LLM call so the cover letter doesn't contradict them (e.g., both say '5 years of React' or both say '3 years'). Implement by calling `generateScreeningAnswersForJob` first (or using the result passed in if `buildPayload` calls both) and including the answers in the cover letter prompt

Add streaming: `generateCoverLetterForJob` accepts an optional `onChunk?: (chunk: string) => void` callback. The buildPayload path exposes `payload.cover_letter_stream` as a `ReadableStream` the extension can consume. For now, the orchestrator returns both the final string and the stream; the extension uses the string and the stream is informational

Validate the cover letter length is in [100, 5000] chars. If outside, retry once with a length-correcting prompt. If still outside, throw `CoverLetterValidationError`; buildPayload catches and falls back to `autoApplicationDefaultCoverLetter`

Add 3 unit tests in `services/ghostwriter.coverLetterQuality.test.ts`: structured output returns 3 paragraphs; contradiction check (screening says '5 years React', cover letter draft says '3 years' → re-prompt); length <100 chars triggers retry
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: `autoApplicationDefaultCoverLetter` is set to a 200-char string and the LLM fails → `buildPayload` returns that 200-char string as the cover letter (no throw)

stories.title: Hardening US-008: defer application row creation, add stale-payload cleanup
stories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the application row to be created when the extension actually reports the result (not when buildPayload runs), and a cleanup job for rows stuck in `ready_for_review` for too long, so crashed extensions don't leave ghost rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Change `buildPayload` to NOT create the application row. Instead, return the payload (fields, cover letter, screening answers, PDF) WITHOUT touching the `applications` table

Move row creation to `recordQueueResult` (in `services/applications.ts`, called by the new `POST /api/applications/queue-result` endpoint in US-011). On `outcome: 'submitted'`, create with `status: 'submitted', submittedAt: now, confirmationId: <from msg>, fieldSnapshot, answersSnapshot`. On `outcome: 'skipped' | 'failed'`, create with `status: <outcome>, errorMessage: <reason>`. On `outcome: 'submitted' | 'skipped' | 'failed'`, set `jobs.lastApplicationId = <new application id>` and `jobs.autoApplicable = false` (job is no longer in the queue)

Add a cleanup job `cleanupStalePayloads` in `services/applications.ts` that runs on server startup AND every 1 hour via a `setInterval`. It marks any application row with `status = 'ready_for_review'` and `updatedAt < now - 1 hour` as `status: 'skipped', errorMessage: 'stale payload'`. Since US-008's buildPayload no longer creates rows, this cleanup is for rows created by older builds (defensive) and for rows created by other paths

If `findByJobId(jobId)` finds an existing application row in `ready_for_review` for the same jobId when `recordQueueResult` runs, update it (don't create a new one) — idempotency for the case where the extension reports twice

Add 2 unit tests in `services/applications.recordQueueResult.test.ts`: `submitted` creates a row and updates the job; `skipped` creates a row with the reason; idempotency on a second call for the same jobId
biome ci . and orchestrator check:types exit 0
Negative case: `recordQueueResult` for a `jobId` that does not exist in the `jobs` table throws `notFound` (404)

stories.title: Hardening: settings type drift guard
stories.description: As a developer, I want a test that fails CI if a setting is added to `settings-registry.ts` without a corresponding entry in the `AppSettings` interface (or vice versa), so the type drift that US-008 had to fix manually doesn't happen again.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `shared/src/settings-registry.drift.test.ts` that: imports the `SETTING_DEFINITIONS` array from `settings-registry.ts` and the `AppSettings` interface from `types/settings.ts`; asserts that every `def.key` in the registry is a key of `AppSettings`
Add `shared/src/types/settings.drift.test.ts` that asserts every key in `AppSettings` is present in the registry (reverse direction)
The tests use TypeScript's `keyof typeof` and `keyof AppSettings` for the comparison so they stay accurate when the types evolve
If the test fails, the error message lists the missing keys (e.g., 'autoApplicationDefaultCoverLetter is in settings-registry.ts but not in AppSettings')
biome ci . and shared check:types exit 0
Negative case: temporarily adding a `dummy` key to `settings-registry.ts` and running the test fails with 'dummy is in settings-registry.ts but not in AppSettings'
